title: platanthera bifolia subsp. brachyglossa
intro: Platanthera bifolia subsp. brachyglossa, also known as the lesser butterfly orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is primarily known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The lesser butterfly orchid has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the lesser butterfly orchid, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, are used in traditional medicine to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
